chapters.title: STAVE V: THE END OF IT

samples.master: Oh, glorious, glorious!

samples.master: No fog, no mist; clear, bright, jovial, stirring, cold; cold, piping for the blood to dance to; Golden sunlight; Heavenly sky; sweet fresh air; merry bells.

samples.master: "What's to-day!" cried Scrooge, calling downward to a boy in Sunday clothes, who perhaps had loitered in to look about him.
samples.translation: "Was ist heute, mein Junge?" sagte Scrooge.

samples.master: "I haven't missed it. The Spirits have done it all in one night. They can do anything they like. Of course they can. Of course they can. Hallo, my fine fellow!"
samples.translation: "I, warum denn nicht," antwortete der Junge.

samples.master: Scrooge inquired.
samples.translation: "Was, der so groß ist wie ich?" antwortete der Junge.

samples.master: "A remarkable boy! Do you know whether they've sold the prize Turkey that was hanging up there?--Not the little prize Turkey: the big one?"
